tasks.chapterTitle: Talks: Neutrino Physics and Gravitational Waves

messages.content:
### Summary: The First African Conference on High Energy Physics (ACHEP2023)

The First African Conference on High Energy Physics (ACHEP2023), held in Rabat and Kenitra, Morocco, in October 2023, served as a landmark event for the African scientific community. Hosted by Mohammed V University and Ibn Tofail University, the conference aimed to foster collaboration, highlight Africa’s growing contributions to high-energy physics, and support the next generation of researchers [S.6].

**Key Aspects of the Conference:**

*   **Scientific Program:** The event covered a diverse range of advanced topics, including neutrino physics, dark matter searches, physics beyond the Standard Model, astrophysics, cosmology, heavy ions, and future experimental facilities (e.g., Hyper-Kamiokande, DUNE, and ATLAS) [S.6].
*   **Capacity Building:** A central goal was to integrate African scientists into large-scale international collaborations. Sessions focused on infrastructure development and policy decisions to enhance long-term participation in global research experiments [S.6].
*   **Inclusivity:** The conference emphasized gender balance and diversity, providing a platform to empower underrepresented groups within the physics community [S.6].
*   **Scientific Recognition:** The event received patronage from His Majesty King Mohammed VI and endorsement from the International Union of Pure and Applied Physics (IUPAP), underscoring the international significance of the initiative [S.8].
*   **Research Highlights:** The published proceedings document significant theoretical and experimental work. For example, research on "Neutrino Spin Light" explores neutrino propagation in moving matter, revealing how external conditions—such as those found in ultra-dense astrophysical environments—influence neutrino energy and radiation efficiency [S.18, S.19].

This conference underscores a commitment to advancing science, technology, and international partnerships, serving as a catalyst for future creativity and collaboration in the field of high-energy physics [S.7, S.8].

Lesson 7 — The First African Conference on High Energy Physics - Presentation5 slides
ACHEP 2023: The First African Conference on High Energy Physics
  • Landmark conference held in Rabat and Kenitra, Morocco, October 2023.
  • Promoted scientific collaboration, gender balance, and diversity in physics.
  • Empowered the next generation of African physicists through global integration.

The First African Conference on High Energy Physics (ACHEP2023), held in Rabat and Kénitra, Morocco, from October 23–27, 2023, served as a landmark event for the African scientific community [S.1, S.4, S.6]. Published as Volume 425 of the Springer Proceedings in Physics, the conference documents state-of-the-art research and fosters international collaboration [S.2, S.3, S.7].

Key aspects of the conference include:

Scientific Scope: The program covered a broad range of topics, including neutrino physics, dark matter, cosmology, quark and lepton flavor physics, detectors, and physics beyond the Standard Model. It featured over 95 oral presentations and 19 poster sessions, highlighting progress in major experiments such as ATLAS, DUNE, and Hyper-Kamiokande [S.6].

Capacity Building and Empowerment: A primary goal was to increase the visibility and integration of African scientists into global research infrastructures. The conference emphasized policy discussions regarding scientific capacity building and firmly prioritized diversity, inclusivity, and gender balance in the field [S.6].

Organization and Patronage: The event was hosted by Mohammed V University in Rabat and Ibn Tofail University in Kénitra [S.6, S.8]. It received the high patronage of His Majesty King Mohammed VI and support from the Centre National pour la Recherche Scientifique et Technique and the International Union of Pure and Applied Physics (IUPAP) [S.8].

Publication and Access: The proceedings were edited by Yahya Tayalati and Mohamed Gouighri. As an open-access publication under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License, the volume aims to serve as a comprehensive reference for researchers worldwide [S.5, S.7].

This document outlines the contents and contributor profiles for a publication centered on high-energy physics, particle detection, and fundamental research in the intersection of theory and experimentation.

Key Research Areas
The compilation covers a diverse range of physics topics:

1. Neutrino Physics: This is a major focus, encompassing studies on spin states, flavor oscillations in magnetic fields, detection methods for Core-Collapse Supernovae (CCSN), neutrino telescopes (e.g., KM3NeT), and experiments like DUNE, Hyper-Kamiokande, and the Sarov Tritium Neutrino Experiment (SATURNE).

2. Particle Collider Physics: Several contributions analyze data and simulations from the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), including results from the ATLAS, LHCb, and ALICE experiments. Discussions also extend to the physics potential of future facilities like the Compact Linear Collider (CLIC).

3. Theoretical and Phenomenological Studies: Research includes explorations into AdS3 topological gravity, island entropy (maximinext surfaces), and the Weak Gravity Conjecture. Additionally, authors examine models beyond the Standard Model, such as the Two-Higgs Doublet Model (2HDM), inert doublet models, and constraints on massive gravitons.

4. Quantum Information: A segment of the research explores quantum resources, including entanglement, quantum Fisher information, and non-classical correlations in systems involving superconducting qubits and dipolar coupled spins under decoherence.

Overview of Editors
The text concludes with biographies for the editors, Mohamed Gouighri and Yahya Tayalati. Both are distinguished researchers with extensive experience in international collaborations like CERN’s ATLAS experiment and the Hyper-Kamiokande project. Their work emphasizes the global nature of modern physics, the development of sophisticated detection hardware, and the mentorship of the next generation of researchers, particularly within the Moroccan scientific community.

Formatting Note
The document includes technical analyses for specific collider processes, such as the associated production of photons and Higgs bosons, denoted as $e^{-} e^{+}$ collider interactions, and examinations of parameters at energy scales up to $\sqrt{s} = 13 \text{ TeV}$ (pp. [S.9]–[S.16]).

The provided text discusses advanced theoretical concepts regarding neutrino electromagnetic properties and their interactions in astrophysical environments.

### Key Points: Neutrino Spin States and Spin Light
*   **Method of Exact Solutions:** The authors employ a modified Dirac equation to describe neutrino propagation in varied external conditions, such as background matter and electromagnetic fields (p. 3).
*   **Physics Predictions:** The approach correctly predicts neutrino energy quantization in rotating matter, impacts on pulsar rotation (the neutrino star turning, or $\nu ST$ mechanism), and oscillations caused by matter currents or polarization (p. 4).
*   **Spin Light of Neutrino ($SL\nu$):** This occurs when a neutrino emits electromagnetic radiation in matter due to its magnetic moment. The efficiency of this process is significantly enhanced when matter is in motion; specifically, the radiation power increases by a factor of $\gamma^2$ as compared to stationary matter, where $\gamma$ is the Lorentz factor (p. 5).

### Key Points: Wave Packet Treatment of Oscillations
*   **Wave Packet Formalism:** To understand long-distance neutrino propagation, the authors use a wave packet approach, which accounts for the decoherence of oscillations during travel through galactic and extragalactic magnetic fields (p. 7).
*   **Magnetic Interaction:** Interaction with a magnetic field ($B$) induces spin and spin-flavour precession and modifies the mass-energy dispersion relation for neutrinos:
$$E_{i}^{s}(p) \approx \sqrt{m_{i}^{2} + p^{2} + \mu_{i}^{2}B^{2} + 2s\mu_{i}\sqrt{m_{i}^{2}B^{2} + p^{2}B_{\perp}^{2}}}$$
*   **Observational Implications:** The interaction between neutrino magnetic moments and galactic magnetic fields can lead to detectable changes in neutrino flavor composition. This suggests that neutrino telescopes like IceCube or KM3NeT could potentially probe fundamental neutrino electromagnetic properties (p. 9-10).

### Detector Networks
*   **Gravitational Waves:** The text briefly notes the current global network of laser-interferometric detectors (LIGO, Virgo, KAGRA, and GEO600) operated by the LVK collaboration, which is dedicated to the detection of astrophysical spacetime ripples (p. 27).

The provided document pertains to the *First African Conference on High Energy Physics (ACHEP2023)*, held in Rabat and Kénitra, Morocco, in October 2023. As the requested page range (P.190-251) falls outside the provided source text (which contains pages v through 55), I have summarized the key aspects of the conference based on the available material.

**Conference Overview and Objectives**
The ACHEP2023 conference served as a landmark event for the African scientific community, organized by Mohammed V University and Ibn Tofail University. The primary goals included:
- Facilitating collaboration and knowledge sharing among scientists, researchers, and students across Africa and the global community.
- Increasing the visibility and motivation of the next generation of African physicists.
- Strengthening scientific infrastructure to better integrate African nations into international experiments.
- Promoting gender balance, diversity, and inclusivity within the field of physics.

**Academic and Scientific Scope**
The conference program featured over 95 oral presentations and 19 poster sessions covering a wide array of topics, including:
- Neutrino physics, dark matter searches, and astrophysics.
- Physics beyond the Standard Model, quark/lepton flavor physics, and strong interactions.
- Electroweak and Higgs physics, heavy ions, and future experimental facilities.
- Capacity building and education outreach programs.

**Key Experimental Collaborations**
The event highlighted updates and research related to major international experimental facilities, specifically:
- Hyper-Kamiokande and DUNE projects.
- ATLAS experiment results.
- Future prospects in collider physics and detector technology, such as advancements for the LHCb experiment.

**Acknowledgments and Support**
The conference operated under the patronage of His Majesty King Mohammed VI. It was supported by the Sponsoring Consortium for Open Access Publishing in Particle Physics and endorsed by the International Union of Pure and Applied Physics (IUPAP). Significant contributions were also provided by the Centre National pour la Recherche Scientifique et Technique and corporate partners such as BERTIN and SUETS.
Here is a quiz for this lesson.

questions.question: What was a primary motivation behind the ACHEP2023 conference beyond sharing scientific research?
options: To lobby for the construction of a new particle accelerator in Morocco
To provide motivation, visibility, and capacity-building opportunities for the next generation of African physicists
To exclusively focus on the history of physics education in North Africa
To compete with existing European and Asian physics conferences for research funding
questions.explanation: The text states that a main idea behind ACHEP2023 was to give motivation and visibility to the next generation of physicists in Africa and to discuss policy decisions regarding capacity building.

questions.question: Based on the content, why is the inclusion of African nations in international scientific infrastructure considered important?
options: It allows African nations to bypass international patent laws
It is a necessary instrument for their better integration into international research collaborations
It reduces the need for local universities to fund their own distinct research programs
It guarantees that all future CERN experiments will be hosted in Africa
questions.explanation: The preface emphasizes that scientific infrastructure is a necessary instrument for the better integration of African scientists into international collaboration.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the scope of topics covered at the ACHEP2023 conference?
options: Strictly theoretical physics, excluding experimental findings
Exclusively neutrino physics and dark matter research
A broad range of subjects including cosmology, Standard Model physics, detectors, and education/outreach
Engineering and infrastructure development with no mention of physics theory
questions.explanation: The conference program included diverse topics such as neutrino physics, dark matter, astrophysics, cosmology, experimental facilities, and even education and outreach.

questions.question: What role did gender balance and diversity play in the ACHEP2023 conference?
options: They were considered secondary to technical scientific presentations
They were ignored, as the conference focused solely on physics results
They were a cornerstone of the conference, aiming to empower underrepresented groups
They were explicitly rejected to maintain a traditional focus on established researchers
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that a cornerstone of ACHEP was its dedication to promoting gender balance and diversity to empower underrepresented groups.

questions.question: What is the intended purpose of the 'Springer Proceedings in Physics' series for the scientific community?
options: To serve as a comprehensive, up-to-date source of reference produced from timely reports at scientific meetings
To provide a permanent archive for all undergraduate physics lecture notes
To act as a peer-reviewed journal that only accepts original, non-conference experimental data
To provide travel funding for students attending international physics meetings
questions.explanation: The series is described as being devoted to timely reports of developments in physics, typically based on material presented at scientific meetings, serving as a comprehensive source of reference.

cards.back: The African Conference on High Energy Physics.

cards.front: When and where was the first ACHEP conference held?
cards.back: October 23-27, 2023, in Rabat and Kenitra, Morocco.

cards.front: Who served as the editors for the ACHEP2023 proceedings?
Name at least three topics covered at the ACHEP2023 conference.
cards.back: Neutrino physics, dark matter searches, physics beyond the Standard Model, quark/lepton flavor physics, strong interactions, or cosmology.

cards.front: What were three major international experiments highlighted at ACHEP2023?

cards.back: The International Union of Pure and Applied Physics (IUPAP).

cards.front: What was a major goal of ACHEP2023 regarding African scientists?
cards.back: To provide motivation, visibility, and capacity building for the next generation of African physicists and integrate them into international collaborations.

cards.front: Under whose patronage was the first edition of ACHEP held?
What was a core dedication of the ACHEP conference stated in the preface?
cards.back: The promotion of gender balance, inclusivity, and diversity.

cards.front: In which Springer series are the proceedings of ACHEP2023 published?

slides.title: ACHEP 2023: The First African Conference on High Energy Physics
bullets: Landmark conference held in Rabat and Kenitra, Morocco, October 2023.
Promoted scientific collaboration, gender balance, and diversity in physics.
Empowered the next generation of African physicists through global integration.
slides.notes: This conference served as a pivotal moment for the African scientific community, fostering international partnerships in high-energy physics.

slides.title: Conference Scope and Scientific Program
bullets: Covered neutrino physics, dark matter, and beyond-Standard Model theories.
Featured major international experiments like Hyper-Kamiokande, DUNE, and ATLAS.
Included 95 oral presentations, 19 poster sessions, and keynote addresses.
Highlighted research in astrophysics, cosmology, and future experimental facilities.
slides.notes: The program spanned critical modern topics, ensuring participants were updated on both theoretical advancements and massive experimental infrastructure.

slides.title: Neutrino Electromagnetic Properties
bullets: Key to understanding neutrino propagation in complex astrophysical environments.
Utilizes the 'method of exact solutions' to account for background matter.
Magnetic moments are limited by reactor, solar, and dark matter experiments.
Theoretical framework involves modified Dirac equations for neutrino interaction (p. 18).
slides.notes: Researchers are increasingly focused on neutrino electromagnetic characteristics, specifically their magnetic moments and interactions within dense media.

slides.title: Predictive Phenomena in Neutrino Physics
bullets: Neutrino energy quantization observed in rotating stellar matter.
Millicharged neutrinos impact pulsar rotation via a new mechanism.
Spin-flavor oscillations engendered by transversal matter currents (p. 19).
Spin Light of Neutrinos (SLν) predicted in ultra-dense environments.
slides.notes: These theoretical predictions, such as the Spin Light of Neutrinos, provide new pathways for probing extreme conditions in neutron stars and gamma-ray bursts.

slides.title: Formalism of Neutrino Spin Light (SLν)
bullets: SLν occurs in dense matter due to neutrino magnetic moments.
Matter motion affects SLν photon emission characteristics (p. 19-20).
Dirac equation solutions provide specific energy-momentum relationships.
Total rates calculated for neutrino helicity transition processes (p. 20).
slides.notes: By applying the exact solution method to transversally moving matter, physicists can quantify the power and rate of electromagnetic radiation emitted by neutrinos.
